text.title: Product Development Trend Axes
text.summary: Four primary trend axes for modern product development practice with evidence requirements and key research output types.

rule.statement: Anchor every trend claim to a primary source with a publication date no older than 90 days. Reject any claim without a URL.
rule.rationale: Trend reports decay fast. LLM training data is structurally behind real markets. Consumer behavior trends degrade in months; process trends degrade in one to two years.

list.item: AI-augmented ideation — AI scans market data and surfaces weak signals; humans decide which signals are actionable. Enables pattern recognition at scale that would take weeks manually.
Continuous customer feedback — real-time iteration loops replacing fixed quarterly research cycles. Reduces the time between hypothesis and evidence.
Rapid pivots — quarterly adjustment cadence vs annual planning cycles. Lower cost of being wrong; faster response to competitive and market signals.
Cross-functional teams — PM, Design, and Engineering co-owning discovery and delivery. Reduces handoff latency and aligns accountability with outcomes.

section.title: Key Research Output Types
section.p: Trend research produces four types of artifacts for downstream consumption:

list.item: Market Analysis Reports — size, trends, competitive landscape with sourced figures
User Need Assessments — pain points and unmet needs with source citations and frequency counts
Concept Validation Studies — evidence that an idea has a real market before engineering begins
Feature Priority Reports — ranked list of what to build first, based on evidence strength and segment fit

rule.statement: Keep process trends (how teams work) and consumer trends (what users want) in separate sections. Never mix them in a single bullet list.
rule.rationale: Mixed trend lists prevent teams from routing insights to the right function. Process trends drive team-shape and tooling decisions; consumer trends drive feature and positioning decisions. Mixing produces unfocused roadmap inputs.
